deeper_backstories: They survived an older crisis that left debts still unpaid.
Their family history is tangled with an oath nobody admits publicly.
They once failed someone important and are trying to avoid repeating it.
personal_motives: They want security without surrendering local influence.
They hope to turn the region's danger into leverage before someone else does.
They want one unresolved wrong quietly corrected.
hidden_pressures: A private obligation is narrowing their choices.
Someone is quietly demanding more from them than they can safely admit.
They are holding together a fragile arrangement that could break suddenly.
relationship_to_player: They see you as useful, worrying, and increasingly hard to ignore.
They trust your competence more than your judgment.
They treat you like the kind of ally who can fix a problem or worsen it.
ongoing_threads: Their future choices keep brushing against your unfinished work.
They have become a recurring source of leads, caution, and leverage.
Their private concerns are starting to overlap with the region's wider trouble.
prominence_notes: They now matter often enough to shape later leads and decisions.
They have become one of the region's recurring human fault lines.
They are no longer just local color; they are part of the campaign's ongoing thread.
